online_test.description:
Online Test (Coding Round)
Duration - 25 minutes - 35 minutes - 35 minutes
There were 3 coding problems on Hackerrank (proctored). Each question had a specific time limit. Also, It was not allowed to switch between questions. If switched, you can't come back again to the previous question.
Difficulty - Medium-Hard
Verdict - I solved all three with all testcases. I think people who have done 1 full and 1 partial were also selected. Around 20 people were selected in OT.
Thoughts - Questions were medium and I think the reason most people didn't do some question was because of pressure or something. So, my advice would be to remain calm, don't stuck on the same approach if it doesn't seem to work, try new things. Have a pen and paper and try things. Also, don't always have clock in mind as Its very distracting. If you have less time available start writing some brute force solution as soon as possible for some testcases. All the three questions I did I submitted in last 5 minutes so it takes time.
One more thing, I have observed in most OTs, first question is more difficult than second or third question (maybe its just for me but my friends think the same). That's why remain calm if you didn't get the first one and try to do the next ones.
questions:
Given 3 strings, find the smallest superstring (string that has all 3 strings as substring). If there are many, return the lexicographically smallest one.
Hint - Brute force for all 6 combinations.

You have two arrays FavCandy and TotalCandy. Now find the number of subarrays that contains all the FavCandy elements. (FavCandy elements may be or may not be distinct).
Hint - In my opinion, this was the hardest of all the three (because of the implementation problems). This is somewhat similar to a GFG problem of finding smallest substring which contains all the characters of some other string.

Given a matrix. You have to find all the paths from (0, 0) to  (n-1, n-1). You can move from a cell to (right, down, or right-down). Also there are some walls which you cant go to. Also find the largest lexicographical path. (Path is basically a vector of coordinates along the path)
Hint - DFS
No MCQs

interview_rounds.title: Round 1
interview_rounds.description:
3 questions were asked.
Approximately 50 minutes.
1st question = Given a string containing just a, b and c, count all the substrings that has equal number of a, b and c.
Approach - Let me first ask you a different question. If there were just 2 characters what would you do? This is a standard question. You can use something like a HashMap and store difference of counts of a and b.
For this, one difference is not enough so store a pair of difference one between a and b and one between b and c.
For the simpler version you can search it on leetcode or GFG, etc.
Link - https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/substring-equal-number-0-1-2/
Also, I didn't get the whole idea at once. I told him about the simpler case with 2 characters. He then asked extend it to 3 characters and then I came up with this. So, many times interviewers are helpful. Just walk them through your thought process and they will help you.

2nd question = Given a chemical formula like h2o3u5(s3h5(h5k4)5)7. (not necessarily balanced)
Find the number of times an element is there. This looks simple and many of you would immediately think something like stack and it has a stack-based solution but stack of what.
I'll give you a hint - Use stack of map<char, int> (character with its count)
Here I would also mention one thing, I didn't get this approach at first. I first told about stack of pairs but then he asked about some cases and said that you would have to reduce the complexity, using maps reduced that.

3rd question - Given a permutation of 1 to 100 numbers. Randomly 5 numbers were made -1. Find that 5 numbers.
Approaches -
 -> Sort the input. Unsatisfactory!! We want a better one :(
 -> Hashmaps or just a vector of size 100 - space is 100 so we want a better one :(
In the above approach what we are trying to do is make a new array of 100 integers to store the information about their occurrence. So can we use the given input array to store that information.
But How would you do that. Let me teach you something really amazing...
Lets say you have a number - old_num.
You have to convert it into new_num such that you have access to old_num too every time.
Take a number (a big number) N and update it as old_num + new_num * N.
Now this number is, lets say - final_num.
Now, old_num = final_num % N and new_num = final_num / N.
Pretty Amazing right.. To read more about it this, see the second method in this article,
Link - https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/efficiently-merging-two-sorted-arrays-with-o1-extra-space/

Some other things were asked as well
Linking Libraries
Extern keyword

Ans - GFG

other_comments:
Verdict - Selected, along with 2 other students.
I focused primarily on DSA and solved questions on InterviewBit and leetcode. I also gave contests on codeforces as it helps in building logic and speed. I didn't have many or a very big project. I had a small project of algorithms visualiser that I made with React. I was asked some questions about it, but they were basic. I observed that projects weren't given that importance in my interview and the whole experience was based on DSA only.
So, my advice would be to solve as many standard questions as possible. Also Wear Formal, have a decent hairstyle, nothing fancy. Be confident while speaking and speak your thoughts consistently. Be passionate while explaining things. Work on body language as well. At the end of each interview, I was asked if I had any question. I would advice to ask questions specifically for the companies. Your questions should show your interest and maybe avoid asking generic questions. Interviewers were really helpful. So don't be afraid.

Best of Luck! Cheers!

online_test.description:
The OT consisted of 2 questions. We had 90 mins to solve them. No proctoring. Can't submit any solution more than once and no test cases were provided except the 2-3 given ones, so we had to make sure it was passing all the corner cases by ourselves. They were quite easy to solve just had to make sure that it was covering all things. I submitted both in 40-50 mins.
At the end, 37(..20 girls..) from the OT and 8 from the Engage program were shortlisted. It was really biased. CG and Luck is important guys.
questions:
Question: Find total number of subsegments in array whose average is equal to a given number.
Looks easy if you have some CP experience or have just solved some question like count number of pair such that a[j] - a[i] == j - i, where the basic approach is to rearrange the equation and convert it to another form like a[j]-j == a[i]-i and is solved by mapping.

Question: Minesweeper. Given a n*n matrix, and location of all the bombs in a 2*m matrix. Print all the cells in the 2D Matrix, where the value should be 'B' if bomb is present or the number of bombs which are there in all the 8 adjacent cells.
Can be solved easily by brute force. I gave a O(8*n*n) and that worked too. Can also be done by just seeing adjacent cells for only the given bombs.

interview_rounds.title: round 1
interview_rounds.description:
The interview happened in the Microsoft team app + Google jam board + any IDE that I was comfortable in. (45 mins for each round 1 and 2)
In round 1, we started off by discussing about my DBMS project and all the functionalities provided by it. Make sure to use the exact words, I by mistake said "Winner's table" instead of "Ranking table" and got a whole lecture on why Rankings will be better(I just heard everything but later told him that I stored all the ranking and just had a slip of tongue). Next he asked two questions. First clone a Linked List which contains a next and a random pointer and second, Find the LCA in a given BST. Standard question.

interview_rounds.title: Round 2
interview_rounds.description:
The interviewer asked me my fav DS and I replied trees. Then he started of by asking me the difference in BFS/DFS, when each of them is used, asked me to write the iterative and recursive code for DFS and run it on any custom input. (Not a Binary Tree)
He then asked me about how much my code would change if we had a limitation in the stack size  but we had a huge tree and we wanted to DFS("Just an extra ques" he added).
To which I said instead of the common tree structure, I will use General Tree with a parent pointer because then we can have all the siblings with us and we don't need to keep a count of child traversed. I wrote a code explained him my approach and he was quite satisfied.

interview_rounds.title: Round 3
interview_rounds.description:
This was based more on OOP than DSA. No HR ques. He asked me what kind of codes have I written till now. I was not prepared for that ques and just replied codes related to DSA and Competitive programming etc. Then he asked me if I have used OOP concept in any of those. I replied "No sir, there weren't that many chances where I could use them although I do know about how all of them work". Then we discussed almost all the major concepts of OOP, inheritance, polymorphism, interface, abstract, their real world use etc. It ended on a good note.
At last he asked me a really basic spiral matrix traversal ques which I coded quickly and it ended in almost 30 mins.
17 were selected at last. Again seemed really biased.

other_comments:
Stay relaxed. Explain your approach properly. Keep communicating. If you're stuck, don't remain silent, they are always ready to help. Please solve IB ques(and other GFG, LC ques, but at least complete IB) and don't ignore OOP and DBMS :)
Sometimes its all about luck, if that happens please don't get disheartened. I'm sure if you have worked hard your efforts won't get wasted.

online_test.description: It was 1hr pure coding based OT . I have done 2 and half questions out of 3 . Competitive Programming helps .
questions:
Convert base 2 string to base 6
 string length <= 100

You will be given an array A. you need to choose exactly K contiguous subarrays such that each element in A belongs to exactly one subarray and you need to maximize the value Z
Here Z= sum of all minimum values in K subarrays

We need to choose a subset (of length K) of indices such that sum(A)/sum(B)  is maximized . Here A and B are arrays and sum(A) implies sum of elements in A corresponding to those indices in that subset . similarly sum(B)
Subset of indices need not to be contiguous .

online_test.description:
OT - It was a non-proctored OT held on a platform called CodeSignal. I believe it was a 90/120 min OT (Cant remember the exact duration).
We had 4 Questions : 3 were problem solving questions which did not require too much time, 1 was a Graph + DP based Question which ended up being the differentiator. Try to identify the difficulty as fast as you can and handle them accordingly. Try to get as many "points" as you can, ideally need to solve everything :)
questions: Did not find it online :(

interview_rounds.title: Round 1
interview_rounds.description:
Coding Round: It was a 45 to 60 min round. We had to solve 1 Qn.
Q) you are given a graph where nodes are cities, there will be thieves in a city and they can affect a radius of upto distance 'k', you will be given a start city and an end city, you will have to return min distance if path is possible, else return -1.

While the question itself is straightforward, the issue was to explain the logic of the problem to the interviewer and having to code it in realtime on your laptop with a set timer of 45-50 mins. This round will be crucial, you essentially go to the next round if you have completed the execution in its entirety (We did NOT know this beforehand). Solve the question as you would but please ensure you finish it and try to write clean and readable code.

interview_rounds.title: Round 2
interview_rounds.description:
Campus Lead Round: It was a 1hr round. It was an aptitude/HR Mix Round which tested your quick thinking and ability to answer questions on the spot!

-> General introductions.
-> Project discussion included asking about one of my projects and my learnings and output from it.
-> My future goal and dream, consecutively follow up questions on how to execute them. Give an example.
-> An aptitude question asking how I would store and query a functionality which I have come to realize is a twist on Geospatial Indexing used by companies to have location estimations efficiently.

It was a Non Coding Round, yet the round is significant as it tests your genuineness and a bit of out-of-the-box thinking. The questions will not be hard perse but the way you answer them will make all the difference.

interview_rounds.title: Round 3
interview_rounds.description:
System Design Round: it was a 1hr 15 min Round. A Rare sighting in Intern Interviews :)

Design a food delivery platform functionality using any language of your choice (C++/Java)
There were a few requirements such as:
1) Create Customer and Delivery partner schema.
2) Find the closest available delivery partner and assign the order to him.
3) Calculate the delivery fee using a fixed formula given.
4) Retrieve the order history of a customer.

There were more nuances as well, It was a round to see how clear you are with building and implementing large scale systems using efficient data structures wherever possible.
Honestly, one of the hardest things I have come across owing to primarily how little of it we practice beforehand. I highly recommend watching a few videos on Youtube to see how it is done. You will have to think it through clearly before you even start to code.

other_comments:
Hi, I am assuming you are here mostly as your Uber OT is coming up or you have been shortlisted for interviews. Good Luck!

If you are here for OT:
A few pointers, CG does matter regardless of the CG Cutoff, Please do not think you have done anything wrong if you were not shortlisted. The questions primarily revolve around GRAPHS and DP, have a good grasp on those!

if you are here after getting Shortlisted for Interview: Well done on the OT :)
We were required to take our laptop for the interviews, so keep it optimized and charged to showcase projects quickly etc.
It is going to be a long day, buckle up and enjoy the process (as much as you can). Never let the energy go down as you have to understand the interviewers will also feel tired and its your duty to make them notice you!
Try and work with your interviewer rather than trying to do it alone, tell him your thought process and ask what they think in a subtle way.

Uber's interview has a notion of being one of the harder interviews across the board, but do not let that affect you. Imagine it being your chance to prove yourself at the highest stage :)

Final Tip: Always wait for the Final Selects shortlist to come out :) and who knows! Just like I had the opportunity to be, You could also be the newest intern for Uber!

interview_rounds.title: Round 1
interview_rounds.description:
The interview started with my intro and then he asked to brief all the projects that I have mentioned in my resume.
After that, he asked me to explain more about my DBMS project. Then he asked few questions on the other projects too.
Then he gave me a coding question and asked me to share the screen and code in some ide. I preferred vscode.
The question is:- Write a function to generate all possible n pairs of balanced parentheses.
once he gave the question I took a min break and gathered the whole logic in my brain and then started coding.
At first, I made a small mistake and when he asked me to dry run my code, I found that bug and fixed it. then it gave the correct output.
after that, he asked me if I have any questions for him. then I asked 2 questions and he answered.
That's it interview done in 25mins :)

interview_rounds.title: round 45
interview_rounds.description:
The interviewer is super cool and he started with some general questions and then gave his intro and asked me to give mine.
After that he gave me a coding question and again same as round1 I took 1 min time gathered the whole logic in my brain then coded it and it ran successfully.
The question is:- print all permutations of a given string.
here i discussed with him some edge cases and all.
Then he asked a design question. literally, I didn't even understand what he is talking about and then after few tries, i said I'm not getting any idea.  Then he asked me one more design question i.e, "you made a web page through which you will be sending invitations for a birthday party. by clicking the view button all the top 50 recent accepted invitations should be visible. How will you implement this" again I was clueless but still I asked for 2 mins time and finally told him that maybe we can solve this with a priority queue. then he said if I know the timer concept. I said no and then he started explaining about that. Now again one more design question but this time it's a very well known question and it is "you have a very big database of let's say it contains aadhar details of each person then how would you search for a particular person's details efficiently . how would you design such a system" then I again took 2mins time ( just because to hide the truth that I know this question already) and then I said I would use btree or b+ tree. then he felt satisfied. then he asked me if I have any questions for him. then I asked few questions and the interview is done.
this took around 45-50mins.

interview_rounds.title: Round 3
interview_rounds.description:
They said this is an HR round but sadly it was a pure Technical round :(
first, she started with general questions, and then she gave me a coding question and again same as rounds 1,2.........
question is: find the median of running stream of numbers.
A very well-known question but again I took 2 mins of time and gathered logic.
but first I told her the brute approach and discussed time complexities then again I came up with an efficient solution that is using min and max heaps... Then she asked me to code this. Then I coded and explained to her the logic before and after coding. Then she asked me some oops and java questions.
- what is an abstract class
- about throwing exception in java
- about try catch in java
- about garbage collection in java
- about how memory gets allocated in a computer.
few more questions are there I guess but I don't remember them.
I tried to answer most of the questions.
this interview lasted for about 50-60mins.

other_comments:
1) All I wanna suggest is don't get tensed/pressurized/scared or whatever.
my trick for not being scared/tensed is: If I answer correctly for all the questions then well and good but if I don't answer correctly or if I don't know the answer I shouldn't get tensed because he has no right to beat me or scold me and moreover he is just a stranger and I won't be seeing his face again in my life so I'm not gonna stress myself because of some unknown person. :)
2) If the interviewer asks some known question don't get excited and tell the optimal approach before itself because they are not checking if you know that question already or not. so take some time and act that you are hearing that question for the first time. first, tell brute then go for optimal approach.
3) when he asks for your intro then the ball is in your court so try as much as you can to impress him with your intro. ( i referred to Anmol bhaiya's youtube video on self-intro)
4) Feel free to laugh(smile) in between coz it's is good for health and for an interview too.
5) in all 3 rounds I asked the same 2 questions to all the interviewers when they asked me if I had any questions for them.
i) what kind of projects would be allotted to interns. will they be big scale projects which may roll out to production or will they be some educational project.?
ii) do you have any suggestions for me?
the first question showed that I am very interested in working on live projects.
and by the second question, I understood whether I get selected for the next round or not.
6) Take the first point very seriously because in my second round though I messed up with design questions. when I asked him for suggestions he told me that I'm very confident while answering and I'm free all over the interview so I think that made me go for the next round.
7) last but very important, LUCK matters a lot in cracking interviews. for someone, the interviewer may be a very chilled-out person but for others, he might ask the toughest of tough questions. So if you don't get selected because of an interviewer it's the fault of your luck so don't get depressed. Try hard to beat the luck next time with full preparation.
ALL IS WELL

online_test.description: OT had three questions to be solved in a total of 90 minutes.
questions:
"1. You are given a (n x m) matrix where the element of matrices represent the number of coins. You can start at any cell and are allowed to move to adjacent cells (left, right, up, down) if the value in the adjacent cell is more than or equal to the current cell. What is the maximum number of coins that can be collected?

Constraints
0 <= n <= 1000
0 <= m <= 1000
0 <= mat[i][j] <= 1000

Sample Input
19 16 18
9 8 7
6 4 9

Output
50

Explanation
We can choose the path 7 -> 8 -> 16 -> 19

Approach
Dijkstras with memoization  O(n^2)."

"2. An IP address is given in CIDR format along with a list of IP addresses. You have to print all the IP addresses in the list that lie within the given CIDR range in sorted order.

Sample Input
CIDR = 10.0.0.0/8
IP list = [""10.0.0.1"", ""10.2.7.8"", ""11.0.0.0""]

Sample Output
10.0.0.1 10.2.7.8

Approach:
This question requires you to know about subnet masking, no hints apart from 2 basic sample test cases were given.
The question input was not properly formatted, therefore the question was not considered for evaluation.
"

"3. A number is said to be special if we can divide its digits into two subsets such that the (sum of digits in the first subset) = (sum of digits in the second subset). Example - 110 is a special number as it can be divided into subsets as (1 + 0) = (1) but 111 is not a special number. You are given two numbers A and B and you need to print how many special numbers are there between A and B.

Constraints
0 <= A <= B <= 10^9
0 <= B - A <= 10^6

Sample Input
101
111

Output
2

Explanation
101 and 110 are the two special numbers in the range 101 to 111.

Approach
Check for all numbers between A and B "

Overall the OT was an easy medium, most of the selected students solved 2 (1,3) in 40-60 minutes. For further shortlisting, I think they went for resumes. Good practice of DSA problems from leetcode and interviewbit would be beneficial. CP can help increase speed.

interview_rounds.title: Round 1
interview_rounds.description:
"In the first technical round, after a basic introduction, I was asked a merge interval question.

You are given a vector of pairs with start and end times and have to insert an interval startNew and endNew into the interval array such that no interval is overlapping in the answer array.

eg
input [[1,3][4,5][7,11][14,16][17,21]] with [8,18]
output [[1,3][4,5][7,21]]

Started asking about the question:
Q: Are the intervals in the array sorted?
Q: Are the intervals in the array disjoin?
Q: What if the array is empty?
Q: Do we have negative integers?

After confirming I moved on to the solution,
 linearly we can traverse the array and update the startNew and endNew if current startNew and endNew fall in the interval else insert them in ans. finally insert startNew and endNew in the ans array. He wanted it sorted so first I sorted the answer array but then he said to improve this, I added the constraint in the traverse and did it in O(n).

Then he asked me if it would work on all cases, I said yes.
Then he said to make test cases to run the program against.
Once he was convinced he asked me to be the interviewer and point out where the code can be improved. I said I should've written the comments explaining the conditions I was checking and should've used reference variables instead.

Then he asked me whether I knew the oops concept and proceeded to ask me about polymorphism, its types, and the difference in static and dynamic binding.

Finally, he asked if I had some questions, I asked how was my interview and about my resume.

PS: The question was very easy, the thing he was looking for was how well you can explain your code."

interview_rounds.title: Round 2
interview_rounds.description:
"In the second round, after my introduction, he gave me a question to find all the subarray with sum S. https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/find-subarray-with-given-sum-in-array-of-integers/

After confirming the limits on S and array elements (negative and upper bound)
I gave him a naive n squared approach, moving forward I said I could use the map to significantly reduce the complexity. As I explained my approach he seemed to be satisfied and told me to code it. I went on to code with proper comments and explanations. He made sure to question and confirm what I was doing.
He then asked me can u reduce the time. I confirmed the constraints and said instead of using a map I can use a vector or array to reduce the time. He seemed satisfied.
He then said to completely dry run the code with all the local variables against provided input.

Finally, he asked me if I had some questions.
I asked about the intern experience in the company.
He explained how interns work and the responsibilities they were assigned.
"

interview_rounds.title: Round 3
interview_rounds.description:
"The third and final round was the HR round.
HR was really cool, He asked me about my resume and hobbies, even went on to ask me about my GSoC and my journey with android. Favorite subject, college experience, and pandemic changes were the next things he asked.

Then we discussed programming languages and the advantages of one over the other. He asked me if I participated in any team events and went on to ask how did the team manage the different skillset of the individuals with the tasks at hand. He asked me what kind of books I read and what was my view on startups. Finally, he explained how salesforce works, the team management, and the kool stuff they are working on."

other_comments:
"Do not forget to take a look at gfg archives and leetcode company wise (Search Github) before interview.

Companies are not looking at how quickly or how efficient your code is, they check for understanding and how well you can adapt to the problem.

As I asked him questions about the problem he cross-questioned me what would change if this was not the case, and how would I account for it.

DO NOT GO BLANK AT ANY INSTANT, keep up the verbal communication. Make comments and explain the code you are writing. Keep him engaged. Finally do not hesitate to ask anything about the question.

Believe in Luck,
The harder you work, the more you'll have of it.

Peace."

online_test.description:
The online test was objective.
There were four sections.
1.Mcqs based on DSA specifically on AVL tree and binary tree.
2. Logical Reasoning and analysis.
3.Language section which included Grammar and Passage reading.
4.Mcqs based on Computer Science subjects including DBMS,OOPS and Computer Networks.
It was medium level but the main thing to worry about was time constraint.
Also there was no negative marking.

interview_rounds.title: Round 1
interview_rounds.description:
The first interview started at 9am.
Round 1 was exclusively technical .
First, I was asked to introduce myself.
Then I was asked about my programming language preference which I replied with C++.
The questions and programs that were asked were pretty basic and they are as follows.

2.Sort the array.
3.Reverse the digits of an integer number

You are given three bottles 3 litres(fully filled),4litres(empty),5litres(fully filled).you are supposed to determine if its possible to fill the 4 litres bottle completely also one of the other bottles should be empty.

interview_rounds.title: Round 2
interview_rounds.description:
Round 2 was a bit technical as well as HR.

My resume was examined in this round.I was asked about Database and if I have ever been involved in its use. On which I introduced my web development project and how Django has implementation of SQL as models.

other_comments:
Now whatever I say here is completely my opinion so feel free to skip the things that you feel are irrelevant.
There is nothing much to tell about online test whether its Mcq or programming, You should refer to as many GFG archives as you can and practise a lot.
Giving an interview is totally a different scenario.
These are some points that one should focus on :
1.Be confident.
2. Clean shave
3. Be interactive even if you dont know the answer .
4.Listen carefully and allow the interviewer to speak .
5.Avoid awkward silence.
6.Never bluff.
7.Be thorough with your Resume.

Also you should be able to express your thoughts.According to me they look for your ability to learn rather than how much you have learned because evetually you can learn things when given time but it takes time to develop the ability to learn.

Again feel free to skip the points that you feel are irrelevant though they worked for me.

online_test.description: There were a total of 3 questions which had to be done in 90 mins. The test was proctored.
questions:
You are given a (n x m) matrix where the element of matrices represent the number of coins. You can start at any cell and are allowed to move to adjacent cells (left, right, up, down) if the value in the adjacent cell is more than or equal to the current cell. What is the maximum number of coins that can be collected?

Constraints
0 <= n <= 1000
0 <= m <= 1000
0 <= mat[i][j] <= 1000

Sample Input
19 16 18
9 8 7
6 4 9

Output
50

Explanation
We can choose the path 7 -> 8 -> 16 -> 19

An IP address is given in CIDR format along with a list of IP addresses. You have to print all the IP addresses in the list that lie within the given CIDR range in sorted order.

Sample Input
CIDR = 10.0.0.0/8
IP list = ["10.0.0.1", "10.2.7.8", "11.0.0.0"]

Sample Output
10.0.0.1 10.2.7.8

A number is said to be special if we can divide it's digits into two subsets such that the (sum of digits in the first subset) = (sum of digits in the second subset). Example - 110 is a special number as it can be divided into subsets as (1 + 0) = (1) but 111 is not a special number. You are given two numbers A and B and you need to print how many special numbers are there between A and B.

Constraints
0 <= A <= B <= 10^9
0 <= B - A <= 10^6

Sample Input
101
111

Output
2

Explanation
101 and 110 are the two special numbers in the range 101 to 111.

interview_rounds.title: Round 1
interview_rounds.description:
At first, the interviewer asked to explain about the project.
What is the project about?
What problems did you face while making it?
How did you resolve the problems?

Then I was asked 3 coding questions

1. Find the indices of the pair of numbers whose sum is K.
At first, I told the brute method and the interviewer asked me about time complexity and space complexity and after that asked me to improvise further.
Then I said about the two pointer approach and again I was asked about time and space complexity and if I can improvise further.
So I said we can use hashing and again I was asked about time and space complexity.
Then I was asked to code the two pointer approach.
After writing the code, the interviewer questioned if it will cover all the corner cases.
I again went through the code and ensured cases like empty array, single element in an array were covered.

2. Searching in a pivoted sorted array. (Pivoted sorted array is a sorted array that is rotated about a point)
Example -  4 5 6 7 8 1 2 3
At first, I said we can do it by using linear search and the interviewer asked about the complexity and asked if it can be improved.
Then I said about using binary search to find the pivot point and then applying binary search in each part which the interviewer asked me to code and also said to ensure that all corner cases are covered.
After writing the code, the interviewer gave me some input and told me to dry run on it.

3. You are given a BST and you need to add the sum of all the smaller elements to the node.
At first, I said we can get the sum of the left sub-tree and then do and the interviewer gave an example and told me to find the answer to it.
While doing so, I realized we can simply do inorder traversal and then I was asked to code it.
In the code I had used a global variable so the interviewer asked if I can somehow avoid the use of global variable.
So I said we can use a reference variable instead and he asked about the initial values of the variable.

At last the interviewer asked if I had any questions.

interview_rounds.title: Round 2
interview_rounds.description:
At first, I was asked to briefly tell about the project.

Then I was asked 2 coding questions

1. You are given an array of elements and you need to find the next maximum element in linear time.
So I said an approach using stack and then I was asked to write the code.
After writing the code, the interviewer saw the code and was satisfied with it.

2. Multiply two matrices.
At first, I started to use the normal n^3 method but the interviewer asked me to do it in a more efficient way.
So I said we can use Strassen's Matrix Multiplication and I was asked to code.
I was allowed to see the algorithm for it.
I tried to code but it was taking too much time so the interviewer asked me to just write how the first intermediate matrix will be found and asked me questions regarding the algorithm.
The interviewer wanted to see if given an algorithm, was I able to write a code for it or not.

At last the interviewer asked if I had any questions.

interview_rounds.title: Round 3
interview_rounds.description:
At first, I was asked some general questions. (How are you? How was your day?)
Later I was asked to tell something about myself.
Then I was asked about the project and another coding question.

You are given 2 sorted arrays and you need to merge them (maintaining the sorted order) by traversing the array only once.
After giving it a few tries I was able to get the logic. I wasn't asked to code it.

Then I was asked a puzzle

You have 6 identical balls and a simple balance. One of the balls is defective (it can either weigh more than others or less than others). What is the minimum number of tries needed to find the defective ball?

Then I was asked about what I know about their company.

And at last the HR asked if I had any questions.

other_comments:
The interviewers were all very friendly and helped by giving hints.
Stay calm and keep interacting with them and tell them what approach you are thinking of. Start with a brute approach and keep optimizing your solution.
Prepare OOPs concepts, DBMS and project related questions apart from DSA.

Prepare your introduction very nicely as that would leave a good impression.
Prepare for common HR questions like what are your strengths, what are your weaknesses, what are you doing to improve your weaknesses, what do you know about our company............
Be prepared to answer questions related to your resume. Make sure you are aware of everything that you have written in the resume.
You can even expect coding, OOPs, DBMS and puzzles in HR round apart from general questions.
Some interviewers may put some pressure on you to see how do you work under pressure so keep a cool head and think calmly.

Good luck and prepare well! :D

other_comments:

DSA: Interview bit, GFG, leetcode, you can also try CP on Codeforce, Codechef (cp is optional )

 "

online_test.description:
This had 3 programming questions.
All were medium-hard level if we look on Leetcode or interviewbit.
The questions were sequential and time dependent.
First Question's duration was 25 minutes.
And Other two questions were of 35 minutes each.
questions:
Given three Strings A, B and C. Find the smallest lexicographically string of minimum length such that string A, B and C can be found as substrings.
Input: A = xyyyz , B = xzyz , C = zzz
Output: xyyyzxzyzzz
Input: A = abc , B = bca , C = aaa
Output: aaabca
My Approach: It was finding least common superstring, due to less time i.e., 25 mins,
I came up with brute force solution of forming super string of all possible combinations and then I passed many test cases.

This was the best question of the OT.
Given an array of N candies whose ith candy is of type candyType[i] and also given M favourite candy types.
Find no. of subarrays (contiguous) such that atleast one candy of each of favourites type is there in that subarray.
Example:
Input: candyTypes = [1,2,1] favourites = [2]
Output: 4
Explanation: There are 4 subarrays satisfying condition
[1,2] , [2], [2,1], [1,2,1]
Input: candyTypes = [1,2,1,1,1] favourites = [1,1,2]
Output: 5
Explanation: There are 5 subarrays satisfying condition [1,2,1], [1,2,1,1], [1,2,1,1,1], [2,1,1], [2,1,1,1]
My Approach: This is same as finding no. of subarrays which contains other array in any order. Similar concept to Leetcode's question, smallest string containing other stirng. I was stuck on this for a while but at last minute, I got this one fully correct.

Given an MxN maze where 0 means wall and 1 is path. The question was to find
1) The total no. of paths from (0,0) to (N-1,M-1)
2) The lexicographically largest path provided (i,j) is greater than (p,q), if i>p or if
i==p and j > q
My approach: This question was easy but only required so much patience, the basic idea was to find unique paths in M*N grid complexity using DP method and to do dfs to get the path.
This got me there only, as due to less time. I couldn't complete this fully, but was able to pass majority of test cases.

RESULT OF OT: Total of 22 students were selected and out of whom I was also shortlisted and my first round of interview was scheduled at 11:00pm in the morning.

interview_rounds.title: Round 1
interview_rounds.description:
My interview was scheduled on hackerrank platform which have IDE, whiteboard
and video/text chat room at the same time.

There were two interviewers there- a female and a male. I was first asked to introduce myself; I give a brief introduction. After that for 2 mins I was asked about what I do, my skills and what is my primary programming language and cross questioned why it is my primary language.
Actually, I mentioned backend development as one of skills and on asking framework when I replied Django, she thought I would code in python as it was default IDE selected there. When I answered C++ as my primary language, she cross questioned me, "As you are
Django Python Developer, why isn't python a language of your top priority?"
To which I replied "Because I have been coding in C++ since class 11th and so it's my primary one and about python, I only use python in Development and Programming".

After this the main interview started. She gave me the first question
Which was actually vertex cover problem but on binary tree.
The question goes like,

"You can put cameras on any nodes of binary tree. If there Is camera on certain
node, I can cover the scene of all its adjacent node (simply its parent (if it's there)
and its children (if any)), I was asked find minimum no. of camera that can cover
entire binary tree."

I started with understanding of the problem that what I understood from the
question and then shared my approach what I was thinking and then while
interacting only, I came up with my first solution.
To which He presented a counter example and then I changed the implementation a
bit and he said it seems fine and we moved to other question.
Main thing I wasn't tested if I can make the code run.
But how do I implement it and am I confident about my code and my concept is clear
or not!!

Then my second question was asked.
"Given a number and you have k moves. In one move, you can change one number to any other number. Task is to make the largest palindromic number provided we can have at-most K moves and no. of digits of number should remain same."

So, I started again with discussing and clearing problem from interviewer and then I propose a solution. She gave an example to check if my code worked and it did. And in the second example it didn't gave required output and then I changed a bit code and then she put a new test case and in 4th try, I proposed the final solution. Her all-corner test cases passed and before He was going to shift for third question, I asked if we can go back to first question page, I want to correct my self there and did a small change for minimising no. of cameras and then the third coding question was introduced.

"Given an array of indefinitely large size you have to implement k stacks in that array, provided you can use extra space but can't waste space in original array"
I gave the most general form that we can split array into k parts then ith part can be used for ith stack, to which I was asked not to waste space and k is unknown it will be during runtime only, for example enter 10 in stack 1, 5 in stack 2, 6 in stack 7 etc. So, I was talking to him only how we can implement and then at last I came up with solution where I can use contiguous element of array as stack by storing kth stack and its corresponding indices in vector associated with that kth stack.

I was only made to write code of 2nd question as much as possible and 1st and 3rd were more for checking my way of solving and thinking.
Then I was asked some other questions:

what is Polymorphism, what is different between run-time and compile-time polymorphism. Virtual Functions and Non-virtual functions.

Now She opened my resume and she asked me some questions from my skillset mentioned there.
Q1) How do you use Django for interacting with database and creating new one or sending new one.
I gave a good answer for this one, explaining what is views.py and what is models.py and we create classed to store tables and basically classes are models in Django.

Q2) What is single word for all data handling in Django models.
I couldn't answer this right, I didn't know the word. I thought we call it DBMS.

Q3) What is routing in Django.
I answered this partially correct but she gave me some hints to get me to correct answer.

Q4) How MVT structure works in Django.
Basically, I was asked working of Django in details. I used whiteboard to explain how it does work and told her how urls are mapped and matched.
After this the interview was concluded.

It lasted for 1 hour 25 mins approx.
8 students shortlisted for Round-2

interview_rounds.title: round 90
interview_rounds.description:
Around 90 minutes after my first interview, I got result that I am selected for second technical round of interview.
Again, the platform was hackerrank and there were two interviewers – Both male.
This time they didn't ask for me to introduce and just asked how was I doing and then they proceeded for the questions.
First, he opened my resume and asked me about my DBMS project, what I contributed there. He didn't ask much and proceeded for questions.
The first question he asked was like this
"Given a string formula representing a chemical formula, return the count of each atom."
And the formula is balanced but doesn't guarantee minimal i.e., examples can be H2O, A2(B3((((((D))4H))))) , (A3(A2((SO4)3M3K5)2A2)5 etc.
I first proposed a brute solution using string stack which was right and then he asked me to optimize it. At first I was not getting idea and he only was giving me hints what can be done and then I got 'em and coded the solution using stack of hashMap. "My suggestion for the reader is: Even if you don't know solution or know solution beforehand, first tell him what are you thinking and how it can be done from most naïve
approach and then dive into better optimisation".

"Important Tip: DURING INTERVIEW, WHATEVER YOU ARE THINKING ABOUT SOLUTION MUST COME TO YOUR LIPS, HE/SHE IS NOT THERE TO SEE HOW FAST OR ACCURATELY YOU CAN CODE. HE IS THERE TO JUDGE YOUR SKILLS IN IMPLEMENTING, THINKING AND GRABBING HINTS"
After this he asked me about time complexities of my solution. This was the only coding question; I was asked and then the same interviewer asked me some questions on OOPs.

Q1) What is singleton class and how to implement it?
To be honest, I got confused what is singleton class but he wants it implementation not definition so he added that singleton class is that class which can have only one object. I was asked how I can implement singleton class. I answered it correctly.

Q2) Design a library management system using OOP, where A library can have many sections. Each section can have many books and all book have sub-sections and author.
I was made to code on IDE there and then he asked me many questions about my class
and the way I used inheritence.
Q3) What is different between inheritance and composition?
I answered this and then he cross-questioned by inheritance is used when composition is an option.
Then the command was taken by other interviewer. He first asked me if I am familiar with SQL and DBMS. On affirmative reply, he gave this question.
"Given a table STUDENT having three attributes "ROLL SUBJECT MARKS" where {ROLL,SUBJECT} can be thought of primary key.
ROLL SUBJECT MARKS
1 A 89
1 B 80
1 C 85
2 A 90
…………..
And so on.
Write a query to find topper of class." I wrote this query on the screen and then he asked me if he does some change will the
code give same result and cross questioned. Then he added, Topper of class must have passed in all subject as well and suppose
minimum passing marks is 40. Now change the SQL QUERY accordingly and find topper (if any).
I though for a while and answered this one using some nested queries.
He said why to use nested query why not directly HAVING clause and required condition. To which I replied, The change you are suggesting will surely check if the topper's min marks are more than or equal to 40 or not but it doesn't guarantee who is topper. Because if that person is not happened to be topper, then we will get 0 tuples as answer. But my code would search for next person as well. Then I added one example:
Suppose Roll No. 1 has 100 100 100 39 marks and Roll No 2 has 51 51 51 51 marks The change u suggested will make sure Roll No. 1 is topper or not but my code will also return Roll No. 2 is valid topper. To which he thought for a moment and shifted to next question. Now he opened my resume and under skills mentioned, he too noticed Django, python and numpy fundamentals.
Now question series started:
Q1) Why NumPy array is better than Python list?
Q2) Is it easier to code in Java or Python? (A discussion on this)
Q3) Operator Overloading in Python!
Q4) Override str(…) function in python
Q5) What is linking and loading in compilers and then he asked my how a code is compiled and .out file (in UBUNTU/Mac/LINUX) is created?
Luckily I had partial knowledge about it in UTP Course and answered it in very non-technical manner.
It was to check if I know not about if I have memorized it. Then he asked me the last question.

"Given an External Memory of 1000GB data and 1MB loading memory All numbers are repeated multiple times except 1 and all distributed indefinitely. Use that loading 1 MB memory, find the number whose occurrence is one.
Constraint
Any number can be present.
No guarantee, which numbers, or even consecutively present
like 1 125 4 124 1 123 2 123 4 2 124 125 can have any order
And Numbers can be repeated any times not just twice"
It was toughest question and I didn't know this, but I didn't just give up. I had a lot of discussion.
I kept on proposing various methods like, how by using 1 MB loading memory can sort it using external merge sort.
Basically, this question was to check how much I know about CPU and memory units. He too put input with me and gave me small small hints and then he stopped me after a while saying, it is already more than 1:20 hours and we can end this here and he asked me If I want to conclude the answer in a line or two. I concluded with what I can think most.
And then for another 5 to 10 minutes we talked about company and how corporate world is different from actual academics.
So, after this my interview ended.

interview_rounds.title: Round 3
interview_rounds.description:
Luckily there was no HR Round. Yayy
3 candidates were selected finally.

other_comments:
You need at least three things in any interview:

1) Confidence – You must have it while answering and thinking. You face mustn't answer that you are tensed that you don't know, and wear a small smile all the time during interview, while answering or while even nodding. And don't look away or down while answering the questions.

2) Communication – During the whole interview, just consider thinking with your lips.
I mean don't waste time in thinking on your own in mind, even if you are getting very naïve/brute solution or no solution at all, don't ask for passing the question.
Just you can say, right now you are not getting correct approach, but I guess we can do (or optimize) this using this and that data structure and algorithm.
Have a good chat and interaction with interviewer, don't make him feel either of you are alone. If you are writing code, tell him why is that line u are coding.
Before you approach for solution, first tell him what's your approach and why have u thought that only. Don't directly jump to solutions, as this might pass message, you knew the question before hand.

3) Acceptance – You should have patience in answering and have knowledge of the same
(obviously). Do not argue with interviewer, (there is different between gentle debate/discussion and argument), sometimes it is better to accept what he is saying.
And you can say, "thank you for enlightening me and adding to my knowledge".
And at last, avoid asking those questions which can be found on internet so easily.
Ask about him and company together.
In round 1, I asked – "How is it feel like working in DE Shaw and what difference you can quote had you been in some other place or other position in same company?"
In round 2, I asked – "As you quoted, you are working with DESHAW for a while, and as we both know, the frameworks and tech stacks you guys use at work are not the ones we are taught in college, so how was it for you to cope up with new tech stack when you joined DE Shaw and can you quote about working experience of YOURS over there?"

[All these tips were given to me by my seniors ]

online_test.description:
1.Microsoft OT was quite easy. It was held on Codility platform. The OT had two questions. It was not proctored and plagiarism check was done by the company (not the platform). The platform had some basic plagiarism checks though.
2. Apart from that having a good CGPA till 4th semester would also help in getting shortlisted.
questions: Count all the subarrays of an array whose average is equal to X.

A N X N 2D matrix is given and it has bombs in some cells . Two arrays of same size are given named as rows and columns. The ith index tells about the cell under which a bomb is present. You have to print a N X N 2D matrix where you have to print 'B' where bomb is present and for the cells where bomb is not present print number of bombs in the neighbourhood .
The neighbourhood of a cell (i,j) is all the cells in the 8 directions adjacent to it i.e. (i-1,j-1) (i-1,j) ,(i-1,j+1) ,(i,j-1),(i,j+1),(i+1,j-1),(i+1,j),(i+1,j+1).

For example -
N=3
rows[]={0,1,1}
columns[]={1,2,0}
Output-
2B2
B3B
1 2 1

interview_rounds.title: Round 1
interview_rounds.description:
Interview was easy. Interviewer introduced himself and then asked for my introduction . After that he asked me about my projects and asked some questions on oops and dbms (my project was based on DBMS) . Then he started with the coding part of the interview.

My first question -> Given a string and a pattern , find how many times the pattern is present as a subsequence in the string.

Main thing is interviewer wants to know how you think . So think loudly and ask questions like what are the constraints of this question (asking this suggests that you not only focus on solving the question but you also bother about time complexity of your solution , as a DP solution would also be possible if string size constraints are appropriate). So after asking this ,he told me not to worry about constraint and just wanted to know my initial approach.  So I told him my recursive approach to solve this problem. He jumped to the next question.

I would also suggest to go through string and pattern matching algorithm (KMP ALgorithm and Rabin Karp ALgorithm).

For Example -
String ="subsequence"
pattern="sue"
Count(answer)=7'

My second question-> Given a string with spaces you have to reverse each word inplace and print the changed string.

For Example - "Welcome to NitW"
So convert the string to "emocleW ot WtiN"

Here key point was taking string input with spaces .I pointed out to him that writing only cin>>string will not take the whole string. So we have to use getline function. Pointing this out may seem silly but do that because may be he would be wanting you to look into that matter. When the questions asked are easy you could be judged on any point so tell even the silly thing.

Then after this my interview was over. For all the questions I was asked to run the code and pass the test cases given by the interviewer.

interview_rounds.title: Round 2
interview_rounds.description:
Interview was easy again. After the intro was done the interviewer jumped directly into the coding. I was asked to code in the Codelity platform(codepair method).

First Question -> Given an array count the number of subarrays with sum equal to zero.

Ques Link -https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/number-subarrays-sum-exactly-equal-k/

I explained all the methods that can be done i.e. from O(n^3) time complexity to O(n^2) and then to O(n) . I didnt take much time while explaining the first two brute force solution and told them in a nutshell. Then told about the O(n) solution. Then I was asked to write some test cases to test my code as well. The scenario was if a code was given what test cases you will take care of and why.

Second Question - Given an array of non-negative integers nums, you are initially positioned at the first index of the array.
Each element in the array represents your maximum jump length at that position.Your goal is to reach the last index in the minimum number of jumps. You can assume that you can always reach the last index.

Input: nums = [2,3,1,1,4]
Output: 2
Ques link - https://leetcode.com/problems/jump-game-ii/

Here I dry ran the given sample test case first and explained how the answer of the sample test cases came. Then I jumped into explanation of the logic behind this question. Then I was asked to test the code by generating test cases. The scenario was same as above.Then he ran the code in some test cases.

After that he asked me some oops question.
What is Polymorphism?
Types of polymorphism (how they are achieved in JAVA).
Then I was asked to write a code explaining inheritance, and types of polymorphism too.(You can use your preferred language).

interview_rounds.title: Round 3
interview_rounds.description:
This was Technical/HR round. It was more of a one -to- one discussion about a topic . I would say you never know how HR rounds would go. I was asked to think as big and broad as possible on a single question.
The question was - if I was implementing Google Drive what functionalities I would have and how will I implement it.(I was not supposed to go deep into the implementation part, but only to give ideas).I explained my idea about parallel copying, chunking(dividing original file into many parts). I was asked to think more and on a broad scope. Then after a few more hits at the question I ran out of ideas and told that to interviewer. This question was point of discussion for maximum time of my interview .Then I was asked how do I handle failure and how I have worked in a team before and experience about that. My interview was over.

other_comments:
Tips-
1. In technical rounds explain using pen paper or whiteboard of your platform(so practice drawing and explaining in whiteboard).
2.Be confident and keep a gentle smile throughout the interview without getting affected ,whether you solve a question or not.
3.Revise DBMS and oops well before the intern drive , these two major are deciding factors.
4.Prepare HR questions ,research about the company a bit and prepare introduction well(this is the part which can make you shine).

online_test.description:
Given 2 coding questions
Time : 90 mins
Website : Codility
questions:
Input : array A of N integers,integer S
Output : count of sub arrays of A with arithmetic mean = S
Constraints :
1<=N<=100000
-10^9 <= S <= 10^9

Input : you will be given a NxN grid and position of bombs

Output : you need to print 2d matrix, such that value of every element which doesn't contain bomb is number of cells among its 8 neighbors which are bombs, where as for element which contain bomb you should print 'B'.
Constraints : 1<= N <= 20

It was basic grid question, which was just implementation.
Anyone with basic programming skills can solve this problem. If u want to optimize your code further you can also do that but with given constraints brute force would work.

interview_rounds.title: Round 1
interview_rounds.description:
Intro
Asked to explain project and asked related questions.
Coding question : generate all possible paranthesis (Recursion and Backtracking)
Asked me if I had any questions.

interview_rounds.title: Round 2
interview_rounds.description:
Intro
Q-1 : Given a scenario where a system keeps triggering some message every minute and also I will be getting request from users like call after 5 min , call me after 10 min. Accordingly I need to redirect system message to the users respectively.
He asked me with my current knowledge how would I approach and what will I do and later asked me to code my approach.

Q - 2 : Finding Running median. (Using min and max heap)

Asked me if I had any questions.

interview_rounds.title: Round 3
interview_rounds.description:
Asked me which languages I know and how was the coding journey so far and what all data structures I know.
Then he asked what is need of linked lists.
What is difference between linked lists and arrays. (In terms of memory and manipulation)
Then he gave me a question to add two numbers represented as linked lists(left to right) and asked me to handle all corner cases (when one of number is negative and when both are negative and so on)
and also I need to store the answer in the larger linked list. (Without extra space)

other_comments:
In Interview : In my case , dbms and oops were not asked ,tech rounds were purely based on dsa,coding.
These are some tips for interviews from my side -
Always start with brute force approach, don't jump to optimized solution even if you know answer before hand. And also explain what is the need to optimize the code.
Just be confident while answering. Every interviewer is ready to help you when you are stuck. So, just go on speaking what you are thinking. Mostly, the person there is gonna see the way you approach any problem and how do you handle corner cases but not just to judge you on how quick and correctly you solve a problem. So, take your time and give your best!!

Sometimes, you may not be able to clear some ot's and also can get rejected after either round 1 or round 2 or even hr, but always remember you have your day so never stop trying!!

Believe me,the moment you succeed after many rejections is unforgettable!! :)

Notable points :
1) Prepare for intro before going for interviews.
2) Be ready to answer any kind of questions related to your projects.
3) Always explain your approaches before jumping to code.
4) Have your questions ready and don't say that you don't have any questions or ask any silly questions. Ask questions like how was ur experience so far in the respective company,etc..
5) Finally, DON'T GIVE UP!! ;)

online_test.description: Total 3 questions to be done in 90 mins . Each que was of medium difficulty .
questions:
"Given matrix (N x M) of coins , you need to find the longest path with increasing coins value . you can only move on adjacent cells ( up, right, down, left ).
 It can be done by using dfs and DP . "
Que was related to  IP address , I didn't understood the que so I skipped this one .

"Find all numbers in the given range [L , R] that are special , a number is said to be special if you divide the digits into two subsets of equal sum.
It can be done using DP ( 0-1 knapsack) ."
26 were shortlisted for interviews .

interview_rounds.title: round 15
interview_rounds.description:
"First of all He asked me to introduce yourself. Always be ready with the answer of this question , make a note of points that you gonna say in interview .
Then he asked me a simple dsa que . QUE - Find all the possible subsets of given array .
INPUT : [1, 2]
 OUTPUT : [ [ ] , [1] , [2]  , [1, 2] ] .
It was pretty simple que of back tracking . He first asked me what will be the total number of subsets of array of length n. After writing recursive code he asked to dry run the code with example [ 1, 2, 3 ]  and write down values of variables for all the function calls  .
Then he asked to prove how total subsets will be 2^n for array of length n . I proved with the help of tree .
He asked if I had any ques and I asked about the company and what projects he is working on , we talked about this around 15 mins ."

interview_rounds.title: Round 2
interview_rounds.description:
"In second round he directly jumped to dsa que and this time que was similar to the que of previous round .
 Given a string ""1a2b"" convert at least 1 letter of the string to uppercase and return all possible strings with given string .
INPUT : ""1a2b""
OUTPUT : [ ""1a2b"" , ""1A2b"" , ""1a2B"" , ""1A2B"" ] .
This one can also be done using back tracking . After writing the code he asked to take a string and dry run the code for all function calls .
Then he asked me to   explain my project , I told everything about project , how does it work , what are the functionalities and all . Then he asked me to convert the models of django into the database in table format , I used white board to explain the database and all the foreign keys in the tables  , then he asked to write SQL query on that table and also optimize it but I was not able to write the optimized query and he told the ans for it .
Then I asked about the work culture of company and how is the work life balance .  "

interview_rounds.title: Round 3
interview_rounds.description:
"It was HR round . He said not to take this as interview but as a interaction .
First we  had introduction then we discussed about the project and what other new functionalities I can add on to my project and how would I implement it.
Then he asked about the work in WSDC and how are the team members and what would you do if one of your team member was not able to complete his task in time .
Then I asked some ques related to company and how is the work from home experience .  8 students were selected for SalesForce ."

other_comments:
"The only thing is to be confident and try to give all the approaches you can think of at the time of interview .
Interviewers are very friendly they will give you hints if you are stuck so don't panic. Prepare for HR round there are some standard ques of HR round on GFG , go through the and be ready with your answers .
GOOD LUCK !!"

online_test.description: 2 questions were given. The time duration was 90mins. Easy difficulty problems.
questions: Given a value k. Count the number of subarrays whose mean is k.
Given a 2d board similar to minesweeper, and locations of bombs. For each cell without a bomb, count the number of bombs in its surrounding cells(8 directions).

interview_rounds.title: Round 1
interview_rounds.description:
He asked me to introduce myself. Then he asked a couple of questions about the projects I mentioned in my resume. He asked me how did I do performance testing on my web projects, how many users can simultaneously use the website and etc, I was clueless since I didn't do any of those and I have said the same to him.

Coding question:
   In any search engine, when we start typing, it will give us suggestions for auto-filling. Given an array of search titles(strings), and a searchWord(string), print 3 lexicographically smallest search titles with the same prefix as searchWord.
My approach :
  I suggested we need a data structure that can search a dictionary of words based on prefix easily, and one well-known data structure for the same purpose is a TRIE. I said that and started implementing complete code from scratch. After some debugging and testing with test cases given by the interviewer, he was satisfied with the code.

Then he asked me about cloud-computing, I have said whatever I knew then he questioned back some more and I wasn't able to say.
Next, he has asked me about test-driven development, once again I was clueless about this, but he didn't seem much concerned.
I think he was just trying to see how would I answer to the unusual questions.

After about 50mins I got notified that I was selected for the final round.

interview_rounds.title: Round 2
interview_rounds.description: I didn't have 2nd round.

interview_rounds.title: Round 3
interview_rounds.description:
This round was HR round(Not mentioned anywhere but it was pretty obvious).
The interviewer joined the meeting and started introducing himself.
Even in this round, the interviewer started off by asking me about my projects. He gave some feature requirements in the project and how would I add that functionality.
After discussing for a while about projects, he asked me this :  "Take any piece of software or application you use daily. Mention any feature in that application which irritates you, and what would you do to improve it".  I gave him a good answer since I actually had one feature which irritated me in mind. I have opened MS Paint and gave a bried explanation about what the current functionality is and what improvements I would do it. He was satisfied with the answer.

Then, he gave me a simple question on Linked Lists : Given a head pointer to a linked list, and a value, delete all the nodes in the linked list which have the same value as the given value. I started my explanation by considering adding a dummy-node in front of the head, but he asked me not to use dummy-node since we shouldn't waste memory.  After coming up with a different approach, he gave me a test case and asked me to dry run with it. During the dry run, I kept on finding some mistakes in the code and when I was updating the code he kept on asking what each of the change I am making to the code is doing.

Next, he asked me why would I want to work at Microsoft.
Then he ended the interview by asking me if I have any questions for him.

Overall, I like this round quite a lot since it was very interactive.

other_comments:
If you mention any projects in your resume, make sure you have good knowledge on it.
The questions are actually simple, but what they will see is how we are approaching the question and explaining them.
Give an honest answer to HR questions.
